summary: Sergey Rodionov evaluates a coding-agent system for ARC-AGI-3 that uses verifier-driven executable Python world models to solve games without hand-coded logic, achieving a mean RHAE of 32.58% across 25 public games.

blocks.content: # Executable World Models for ARC-AGI-3 in the Era of Coding Agents

source_span.quote: # Executable World Models for ARC-AGI-3 in the Era of Coding Agents

blocks.content: We evaluate an initial coding-agent system for ARC-AGI-3 in which the agent maintains an executable Python world model, verifies it against previous observations, refactors it toward simpler abstractions as a practical proxy for an MDL-like simplicity bias, and plans through the model before acting.

source_span.quote: We evaluate an initial coding-agent system for ARC-AGI-3 in which the agent maintains an executable Python world model, verifies it against previous observations, refactors it toward simpler abstractions as a practical proxy for an MDL-like simplicity bias, and plans through the model before acting.

blocks.content: The system is intentionally direct: it uses a scripted controller, predefined world-model interfaces, verifier programs, and a plan executor, but no hand-coded game-specific logic.

source_span.quote: The system is intentionally direct: it uses a scripted controller, predefined world-model interfaces, verifier programs, and a plan executor, but no hand-coded game-specific logic.

blocks.content: The agent fully solved 7 games, achieved a Relative Human Action Efficiency greater than 75%, on 6 games, and obtained a mean per-game RHAE of 32.58%.

source_span.quote: The agent fully solved 7 games, achieved a Relative Human Action Efficiency greater than 75%, on 6 games, and obtained a mean per-game RHAE of 32.58%.

blocks.content: Because the system uses no game-specific code, it can serve as a game-general baseline for ARC-AGI-3.

source_span.quote: Because the system uses no game-specific code, it can serve as a game-general baseline for ARC-AGI-3.

blocks.content: Performance on the private validation set remains to be tested.

source_span.quote: Performance on the private validation set remains to be tested.

blocks.content: Overall, the results provide preliminary evidence that verifier-driven executable world models are a promising approach for ARC-AGI-3 agents.

source_span.quote: Overall, the results provide preliminary evidence that verifier-driven executable world models are a promising approach for ARC-AGI-3 agents.
